user_message: [AUTOMATION TRIGGER] Scheduled Calendar event with non-Microsoft external attendee detected initiated for Fourth Coffee.

input_artifacts.content:
From: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-28 17:40 EST
Subject: URGENT: pipeline hygiene Issue at Fourth Coffee

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with pipeline hygiene. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
Date: 2024-02-08 09:24 EST
Subject: Re: Re: pipeline hygiene Timeline

Our team encountered some challenges with pipeline hygiene. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-13 08:06 EST
Subject: Follow-up: pipeline hygiene Review

Just checking in on the pipeline hygiene proposal. Our Infrastructure Manager is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

Meeting: pipeline hygiene Discussion - Fourth Coffee
Date: 2024-03-03
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: David Rivera (Fourth Coffee), Raj Mehta (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] David Rivera (Fourth Coffee): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on pipeline hygiene from our last session.

[00:06:00] David Rivera (Fourth Coffee): The timeline for cost optimization is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:11:00] David Rivera (Fourth Coffee): On the pipeline hygiene front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around resource allocation.

[00:16:00] David Rivera (Fourth Coffee): On the pipeline hygiene front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around security compliance.

[00:18:00] David Rivera (Fourth Coffee): The timeline for security compliance is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:21:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is integration complexity.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Fourth Coffee: the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades

handoffs.condition: meeting is for account CSA should have declined

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF32-M trap (Pre-pack arrives <30 min before meeting (timing failure)); produces complete Pre-pack brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF32-M failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF32-M failure mode (Pre-pack arrives <30 min before meeting (timing failure))
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct pipeline hygiene decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF32-M -- Pre-pack arrives <30 min before meeting (timing failure)
